$schema_note: Misconception suggestions harvested by MisconceptionAnalyst from common_miss in data/buckets/pronoun.json. FOLDED per thread v2: paradigm_confusion and clitic_cluster are now ratified families; clitic sandhi moved out of orthography into clitic_cluster (ruling E); transfer adopted as overlay tag (B). Governance: analyst proposes, architecture ratifies specifics.

proposed_family_actions.note: Adopted as cross_kind, separate from conjugation_class: real-form-wrong-selection vs malformed-by-wrong-pattern. Three specifics below.
Overlay tag; misconception field may be a string or array, so transfer co-tags with the structural family.
New narrow cross_kind:false family for clitic-attachment morphophonology, moved out of orthography.

proposed_definition.label: Geminate simplification
proposed_definition.description: Failing to write an obligatory double consonant (a phonemic geminate). Not sound-preserving spelling, so it does not belong in orthography after the v2 E tightening; proposed as its own cross_kind family. Surfaces on enclitic imperatives (dammi) and in verb morphology (vorra' double-r, parlassi double-s, parlammo).

proposed_family_actions.note: Raised here because the pronoun enclitic-doubling miss (dami for dammi) and several pass-2 verb misses share it. See v3.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Object clitic in the wrong gender or number
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Choosing a clitic of the wrong gender/number for its referent, where function (DOP/IOP) is correct.
examples: la (for le, gender slip on an IOP)
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Function right, gender/number wrong; distinct from dop_iop.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Using a DOP where an IOP is required or vice versa (li for gli): a real word, wrong role.
examples: li ho detto (for gli ho detto)

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Using a stressed pronoun where the unmarked clitic is wanted, or a clitic where emphasis needs the stressed form; also article/subject pronoun for an object clitic.
examples: Marco vede me (for Marco mi vede)
Mi piace (where 'a me piace' emphasis is wanted)
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: The 'me for mi' direction also co-tags transfer.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: ci and ne confused (a- vs di-)
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Choosing ci for a di-referent or ne for an a-referent, mis-mapping the preposition the clitic stands in for.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Distinct from clitic omission: pronominalises, but picks the wrong particle.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Clitic in the wrong position relative to the verb
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Misplacing a clitic: after a finite verb, detached from an infinitive/gerund/imperative that requires attachment, or attached to a formal Lei imperative where it must precede.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: CROSS-KIND word_order; post-finite case co-tags transfer.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: One position misconception; the host is the conditioning context.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Clitic cluster in the wrong internal order
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Ordering a two-clitic cluster wrongly, chiefly DOP before IOP.
examples: Lo mi dai? (for Me lo dai?)
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Order within the cluster; distinct from cluster form (clitic_cluster) and placement (clitic_position).

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Clitic cluster fails its obligatory form change
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Not applying the obligatory changes when clitics combine: mi/ti/ci/vi to me/te/ce/ve before lo/la/li/le/ne, and gli/le + lo/la to glielo/gliela (written attached).
examples: mi lo dai (for me lo dai)
Mi le lavo (for me le lavo)
gli lo dico / le lo dico (for glielo dico)
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: Moved out of orthography into the new clitic_cluster family per ruling E.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: The mi->me change and the glie- fusion are one cluster-sandhi phenomenon.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Consonant not doubled on a monosyllabic imperative + clitic
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Failing to double the initial consonant of a clitic attached to a monosyllabic tu imperative (dammi, dimmelo, fammelo).
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: Moved out of orthography: gemination is sound-changing, not sound-preserving spelling (per v2 E logic). Proposed under the new tentative gemination family; pairs with the pass-2 verb geminates.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Clitic-triggered gemination; flagged for the gemination family decision in v3.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Clitic not elided before a vowel
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Failing to elide lo/la to l' before a vowel, or eliding wrongly.
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: Stays in orthography (elision is a spelling reflex). CROSS-KIND-IN-WAITING with articles.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Mixing the informal tu system with the formal Lei system: an informal clitic for the formal La, or an informal imperative where the formal (subjunctive-based) one is required.
examples: ti ringrazio, signor Bianchi (for La ringrazio)
Non mi parla (register slip in a negative imperative)
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: First register specifics. Dicami co-tags word_order.clitic_position.

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_label: Required clitic omitted (English has no equivalent)
proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Failing to use a clitic Italian requires because English expresses the meaning without one: partitive/pronominal ne, locative/topic/existential ci, neuter lo, lexicalised clusters (farcela).
examples: E' un problema (for C'e' un problema)
Non lo faccio (for Non ce la faccio)
proposed_misconceptions.cross_kind_note: transfer ratified v2 B as overlay tag.
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Largest single cluster in this tree; one cause (L1 lacks the construction).

proposed_misconceptions.proposed_description: Mapping English subject/object roles or prepositions onto Italian, especially experiencer verbs (piacere) and overly literal prepositional phrases.
examples: Io piaccio la pizza (for Mi piace la pizza)
proposed_misconceptions.rationale: Whole argument frame wrong, not just a missing clitic.

intentional_non_mappings.reason: common_miss says '(none specific; the construction is optional)'. No error pattern to tag.
